mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit designed for Kubernetes, enabling users to build, deploy, and manage machine learning workflows. It is particularly useful for AI practitioners and platform administrators who require a modular and scalable solution for AI projects. Key trade-offs include the complexity of installation and configuration versus the flexibility and power it provides for managing ML workflows.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow CLI, which is essential for managing Kubeflow deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kubeflow CLI for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Fetch credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step installs Kubeflow using the CLI, which sets up all necessary components.

```bash

kubeflow install --version v1.10.0

```

> Install Kubeflow version 1.10.0.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Set up monitoring.
> Set up autoscaling.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes.

```bash

kubeflow uninstall

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Uninstall Kubeflow from the cluster.
> Delete the GKE cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade your Kubeflow installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp <your-pod-name>:/path/to/data /local/backup

kubeflow upgrade --version v1.10.0

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system

```

> Backup your data.
> Upgrade to Kubeflow version 1.10.0.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow install' command fails with 'insufficient permissions'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The user does not have the necessary permissions to create resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your user has the 'Kubernetes Engine Admin' role.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes' returns 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not be created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the GKE console for cluster status and recreate if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'kubeflow-system' are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be insufficient resources allocated to the cluster.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resource limits for the pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow uninstall' fails with 'resource not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubeflow installation may not have completed successfully.

**Fix:**
Manually delete any remaining resources in the 'kubeflow-system' namespace.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new ML frameworks, improved integration with existing Kubernetes resources.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older versions of TensorFlow and PyTorch.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to migrate your existing pipelines to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of running pods in the 'kubeflow-system' namespace when you run 'kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
